scenarios.title: I moved to another city
scenarios.description: What to do when you've relocated

steps.text: Apply for transfer of registration using Form 6 at voters.eci.gov.in
Select 'Shifting from one constituency to another' as the reason
Provide new address proof (Aadhaar, utility bill, rent agreement)
Your old registration will be deleted after new one is approved
Processing takes 2-4 weeks — apply well before election deadlines

scenarios.source: Election Commission of India — Form 6 Guidelines

scenarios.title: I lost my Voter ID
scenarios.description: How to get a duplicate or use alternatives

steps.text: Apply for a duplicate Voter ID using Form 001 at voters.eci.gov.in
You can also download e-EPIC (digital voter ID) instantly
On election day, you can use any of 12 approved photo IDs instead
Approved alternatives: Aadhaar, Passport, Driving License, PAN Card, etc.
File an FIR if your ID was stolen — carry the FIR copy as record

scenarios.title: Am I eligible to vote?
scenarios.description: Check if you meet all requirements

steps.text: You must be an Indian citizen (not an NRI with cancelled citizenship)
You must be 18 years or older on January 1st of the qualifying year
You must be a resident of the constituency where you're registered
You must not be disqualified due to unsoundness of mind or corrupt practices
NRIs can vote under Section 20A of the R.P. Act — must be present in person

scenarios.title: I'm an NRI — Can I vote?

steps.text: Yes! NRIs can register using Form 6A at voters.eci.gov.in
You must have an Indian passport (valid or expired)
Registration is for the constituency of your passport address
Currently, you must be physically present to vote — no postal ballot yet for NRIs in general elections
Carry passport + visa as proof at the polling station

scenarios.source: Section 20A, Representation of the People Act

scenarios.title: My name is not in the voter list
scenarios.description: Steps to resolve missing voter registration

steps.text: Search your name at voters.eci.gov.in or Voter Helpline App
Try searching with different name spellings or family member details
If not found, apply fresh using Form 6 (new registration)
Contact your Booth Level Officer (BLO) — they can help verify
Call 1950 (Voter Helpline) for immediate assistance

scenarios.title: I have a disability — What facilities are available?

steps.text: Every polling station must have ramps and wheelchair access
Braille-enabled EVMs are available for visually impaired voters
You can bring a companion to assist you in the voting booth
Priority queue for PwD voters, pregnant women, and senior citizens
Postal ballot facility available for 40%+ disability — apply via Form 12D

scenarios.source: Election Commission of India — Accessible Elections Guidelines
